title: Environmental Engineer

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can draft permit applications, run standard dispersion and contaminant-transport models, but site remediation judgment and regulatory negotiation stay engineer-led.
rationales.toolMaturity: Modeling software with AI-assisted scenario generation exists but is not yet a mature, embedded standard across the field.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is uneven, concentrated at larger environmental consultancies serving industrial clients.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run compliance-scenario modeling and draft reports, but field sampling and agency negotiation require a human engineer on site.

tasksAtRisk: Permit application drafting and standard compliance reports
tasksGrowing: Site remediation strategy and field judgment
Regulatory agency negotiation and public-comment response
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 17-2081, p25-p75 rounded
insight: Permit drafting and dispersion modeling are increasingly AI-assisted, but remediation strategy and agency negotiation still depend on an engineer's field judgment and standing with regulators.
